name: psychopy-bids

metadata.name: psychopy-bids
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: A PsychoPy plugin for using the Brain Imaging Data Structure (BIDS) to organize and describe data.

documentation:
# psychopy-bids

A [PsychoPy](https://www.psychopy.org/) plugin to work with the [Brain Imaging Data Structure (BIDS)](https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/)

* **Website:** [https://psygraz.gitlab.io/psychopy-bids](https://psygraz.gitlab.io/psychopy-bids)
* **Documentation:** [https://psychopy-bids.readthedocs.io](https://psychopy-bids.readthedocs.io)
* **Source code:** [https://gitlab.com/psygraz/psychopy-bids](https://gitlab.com/psygraz/psychopy-bids)
* **Contributing:** [https://psychopy-bids.readthedocs.io/en/latest/contributing](https://psychopy-bids.readthedocs.io/en/latest/contributing)
* **Bug reports:** [https://gitlab.com/psygraz/psychopy-bids/issues](https://gitlab.com/psygraz/psychopy-bids/issues)
* **Code of Conduct:** [https://psychopy-bids.readthedocs.io/en/latest/conduct](https://psychopy-bids.readthedocs.io/en/latest/conduct)

## Installation

We recommend installation in a virtual environment.

```console
pip install psychopy-bids
```

## Usage

The psychopy bids plugin can be used to create valid BIDS valid datasets by creating [behavioral](https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/modality-specific-files/behavioral-experiments.html#example-_behtsv) or [task events](https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/04-modality-specific-files/05-task-events.html) in Psychopy. This can be done directly in python code or using the psychopy builder.

In code, the *BIDSHandler* can create or extend an existing BIDS dataset, including directory structure and necessary metadata files. Individual BIDS events can be added during the experiment and are passed to the *BIDSHandler* to write event `.tsv` files and accompanying `.json` files.

```py
from psychopy_bids import bids

handler = bids.BIDSHandler(dataset="example", subject="01", task="A")
handler.createDataset()

events = [
    bids.BIDSTaskEvent(onset=1.0, duration=0.5, event_type="stimulus", response="correct"),
    bids.BIDSTaskEvent(onset=1.0, duration=0, trial_type="trigger")
]

for event in events:
    handler.addEvent(event)

participant_info = {"participant_id": handler.subject, "age": 18}

handler.writeTaskEvents(participant_info=participant_info)

handler.addStimuliFolder(event_file="example/sub-01/beh/sub-01_task-A_run-1_events.tsv")
```

## Contributing

Interested in contributing? Check out the [contributing guidelines](https://psychopy-bids.readthedocs.io/en/latest/contributing/). Please note that this project is released with a [Code of Conduct](https://psychopy-bids.readthedocs.io/en/latest/conduct/). By contributing to this project, you agree to abide by its terms.

## License

`psychopy-bids` was created by Christoph Anzengruber & Florian Schöngaßner. It is licensed under the terms of the GNU General Public License v3.0 license.

## Credits

`psychopy-bids` was created with [`cookiecutter`](https://cookiecutter.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) and the `py-pkgs-cookiecutter` [template](https://github.com/py-pkgs/py-pkgs-cookiecutter).

modules.name: bids_beh
documentation:
This package provides a PsychoPy Builder component that represents an event of a behavioural
experiment.

documentation: Write code at the beginning of the experiment.

documentation: Write code at the end of the routine.

This class is used for events that do not include the mandatory onset and duration columns.
Events are, for example, stimuli presented to the participant or participant responses.

documentation:
Initialize a BIDSBehEvent object.

Parameters
----------
*args : tuple
    Any arguments that the object's superclass's `__init__` method might require.
**kwargs : dict
    Any keyword arguments that the object's superclass's `__init__` method might require.

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidsbehevent.BIDSBehEvent.__repr__
documentation:
Return a printable representational string of the given object.

This method returns a string representation of the BIDSBehEvent object containing its
attribute-value pairs.

Returns:
-------
str
    A string representation of the BIDSBehEvent object.

A class that represents events of behavioral experiments.

This class is used for events that do not include the mandatory onset and duration columns.
Events are, for example, stimuli presented to the participant or participant responses.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> event = BIDSBehEvent(trial=1, resp='L')
```

# Notes

For more details on behavioral experiment files, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/04-modality-specific-files/07-behavioral-experiments.html>).

modules.name: psychopy_bids.bids
documentation:
This package provides classes that supports the creation of a BIDS-compliant dataset with modality
agnostic files describing the dataset and participants, as well as the creation of modality specific
files describing task events and behavioral experiments.

This class provides methods for creating and managing BIDS datasets and their modality agnostic
files plus modality specific files.

modules.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent
documentation:
This module provides the classes BIDSTaskEvent, BIDSError, OnsetError, DurationError,
TrialTypeError, SampleError, ResponseTimeError, HedError, StimFileError, IdentifierError,
DatabaseError

classes.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a BIDSTaskEvent object.

Parameters
----------
onset : int, float, str
    Onset (in seconds) of the event, measured from the beginning of the acquisition of the
    first data point stored in the corresponding task data file.
duration : int, float, str
    Duration of the event (measured from onset) in seconds.
*args : tuple
    Any arguments that the object's superclass's `__init__` method might require.
trial_type : str, optional
    Primary categorisation of each trial to identify them as instances of the experimental
    conditions.
sample : int, str, optional
    Onset of the event according to the sampling scheme of the recorded modality.
response_time : int, float, str, optional
    Marker value associated with the event.
value : object, optional
    Marker value associated with the event.
hed : str, optional
    Hierarchical Event Descriptor (HED) Tag.
stim_file : str, optional
    Represents the location of the stimulus file (such as an image, video, or audio file)
    presented at the given onset time.
identifier : str, optional
    Represents the database identifier of the stimulus file presented at the given onset
    time.
database : str, optional
    Represents the database of the stimulus file presented at the given onset time.
**kwargs : dict
    Any keyword arguments that the object's superclass's `__init__` method might require.

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.__setitem__
documentation:
Set the value associated with a specific key.

If the key already exists in the event, the new value will be validated using
the `_validateAttribute` method before being set.

Parameters
----------
key : str
    The key for which to set the value.
value : object
    The value to be associated with the key.

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.__repr__
documentation:
Return a printable representational string of the given object.

This method returns a string representation of the BIDSTaskEvent object containing its
attribute-value pairs.

Returns:
-------
str
    A string representation of the BIDSTaskEvent object.

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent._validateOnset
documentation:
Validate and format the onset value.

This method ensures that the onset value is a numeric type or can be successfully converted
to a float. If the value is provided as a string, it must be a numeric string (containing
only digits).

Parameters
----------
value : int, float, str
    The onset value to be validated. Can be provided as an int, float, or numeric string.

Returns
-------
float
    The validated onset value, rounded to four decimal places.

Raises
------
OnsetError
    If the provided onset value is not a valid numeric type or string.

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent._validateDuration
documentation:
Validate and process the duration value.

This method checks the provided value and validates whether it represents a valid
duration for an event. Valid duration values include non-negative integers or floats,
as well as the string "n/a". If the provided value is numeric as a string, it is
converted to a float before validation.

Parameters
----------
value : int, float, str
    The value to be validated as a valid event duration.

Returns
-------
float or str
    The processed and validated duration value.

Raises
------
DurationError
    If the provided value is not a valid event duration.

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent._validateSample
documentation:
Validate and process the sample value.

This method checks the provided value and validates whether it represents a valid sample
index for an event. Valid sample values include positive integers. If the provided value is
numeric as a string, it is converted to an integer before validation.

Parameters
----------
value : int or str
    The value to be validated as a valid sample index.

Returns
-------
int or None
    The processed and validated sample index, or None if the value is not valid.

Raises
------
SampleError
    If the provided value is not a valid sample index.

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent._validateResponseTime
documentation:
Validate and process the response time value.

This method checks the provided value and validates whether it represents a valid response
time for an event. Valid response time values include non-negative integers, floats, as well
as the string "n/a". If the provided value is numeric as a string, it is converted to a
float before validation.

Parameters
----------
value : int or float or str
    The value to be validated as a valid response time.

Returns
-------
float or str or None
    The processed and validated response time value, or None if the value is not valid.

Raises
------
ResponseTimeError
    If the provided value is not a valid response time.

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent._validateAttribute
documentation:
Validate and process an attribute value.

This method performs validation on an attribute value. If the value is not None,
it checks whether it's an instance of string. If it's a string, it is returned as-is.
If the value is not a string, an error class corresponding to the key's name is raised.

Parameters
----------
key : str
    The key representing the attribute's name.
value : object
    The value to be validated.

Returns
-------
str or None
    The validated string value or None if the value is None.

Raises
------
AttributeError
    If the provided value is not a valid string attribute.

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.database
documentation: Represents the database of the stimulus file presented at the given onset time.

A class representing task events.

This class describes timing and other properties of events recorded during a run. Events are,
for example, stimuli presented to the participant or participant responses.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> event = BIDSTaskEvent(onset=1.0, duration=0)
```

# Notes

For more details on task event files, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/04-modality-specific-files/05-task-events.html>).

classes.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSError
documentation:
Base class for all BIDS-related exceptions.

This class serves as the base exception class for all exceptions related to BIDS (Brain
Imaging Data Structure) specifications and operations.

classes.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.OnsetError

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.OnsetError.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the OnsetError instance.

Parameters
----------
onset : object
    The onset value that caused the error.
msg : str, optional
    Explanation of the error.

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidstaskevent.OnsetError.__str__
documentation:
Return a string representation of the error.

Returns
-------
str
    A string containing the onset value and the error message.

Exception raised when onset value is incorrect.

This exception is raised when the provided onset value does not meet the expected criteria
for correctness according to BIDS (Brain Imaging Data Structure) specifications.

A class that represents events of behavioral experiments.

This class is used for events that do not include the mandatory onset and duration columns.
Events are, for example, stimuli presented to the participant or participant responses.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> event = BIDSBehEvent(trial=1, resp='L')
```

# Notes

For more details on behavioral experiment files, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/04-modality-specific-files/07-behavioral-experiments.html>).

modules.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler
documentation: This module provides the class BIDSHandler

classes.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a BIDSHandler object.

Parameters
----------
dataset : str
    A set of neuroimaging and behavioral data acquired for a purpose of a particular study.
subject : str, optional
    A person or animal participating in the study.
task : str, optional
    A set of structured activities performed by the participant.
session : str, optional
    A logical grouping of neuroimaging and behavioral data consistent across subjects.
data_type : str, optional
    A functional group of different types of data.
acq : str, optional
    Custom label to distinguish different conditions present during multiple runs of the
    same task.
runs : bool, optional
    If True, run will be added to filename.

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.__del__
documentation:
Perform cleanup operations when the object is about to be destroyed.

This method is automatically called when the object is garbage collected or destroyed. It
is responsible for performing cleanup actions such as creating a dataset, writing events to
files, adding sidecar metadata, and adding a stimuli folder.

# Note

> This method should be used with caution as the timing of its execution
> is not guaranteed and it may not be invoked in certain circumstances.

# Raises

> KeyError: If there is an issue with accessing dictionary keys during the cleanup
> process.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> del handler
```

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addEvent
documentation:
Add an event to the list of events.

Parameters:

: event: Any - The event to be added to the list.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler.addEvent(BIDSBehEvent(trial=1))
```

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addChanges
documentation:
Update the version history of the dataset.

This method updates the CPAN changelog-like file `CHANGES` by adding a new version entry
with the specified changes and incrementing the version number accordingly.

# Parameters

changes

: List of changes or bullet points for the new version.

version

: The version part to increment. Must be one of "MAJOR", "MINOR", or "PATCH".

force

: Specifies whether existing file should be overwritten.

# Returns

None

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(
...     dataset="/path/to/your/dataset/",
...     subject=None,
...     task=None
... )
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addChanges(["Added new data files"], "MAJOR")
```

# Notes

Version history of the dataset (describing changes, updates and corrections) MAY be provided
in the form of a CHANGES text file. This file MUST follow the CPAN Changelog convention. The
CHANGES file MUST be either in ASCII or UTF-8 encoding. For more details on the CHANGES
file, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/03-modality-agnostic-files.html#changes>).

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addDatasetDescription
documentation:
Add a description to the dataset.

This method adds the required `dataset_description.json` file to the dataset.

# Parameters

force

: Specifies whether existing files should be overwritten.

# Returns

None

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addDatasetDescription()
```

# Notes:

The file `dataset_description.json` is a JSON file describing the dataset. Every dataset
MUST include this file. For more details, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/03-modality-agnostic-files.html#dataset-description>).

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addJSONSidecar
documentation:
Add a JSON sidecar file.

This method adds an accompanying JSON sidecar file to support tabular data file
documentation.

# Parameters

event_file

: The path of the accompanying task event file.

existing_file

: The path to an existing sidecar JSON file.

version

: The software version used in the experiment.

# Return

file_name

: The path of the created json event file.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addJSONSidecar("sub-01_ses-1_task-simple_run-1_events.tsv")
```

# Notes:

All tabular data files MAY be accompanied by a JSON file describing the columns in detail.
For more details on tabular files, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/02-common-principles.html#tabular-files>).

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addLicense
documentation:
Add a license file to the dataset.

This method downloads a license with the given identifier from the SPDX license list and
copies the content into the file `LICENSE`.

# Parameters

identifier

: Identifier of the license.

force

: Specifies whether existing file should be overwritten.

# Returns

None

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addLicense("CC-BY-NC-4.0")
```

# Notes:

A LICENSE file MAY be provided in addition to the short specification of the used license in
the dataset_description.json "License" field. The "License" field and LICENSE file MUST
correspond. The LICENSE file MUST be either in ASCII or UTF-8 encoding. For more details on
the LICENSE file, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/03-modality-agnostic-files.html#license>).

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addReadme
documentation:
Add a text file explaining the dataset in detail.

This method adds a `README` template file to the dataset, which contains the main sections
needed to describe the dataset in more detail.

# Parameters

force

: Specifies whether existing file should be overwritten.

# Returns

None

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addReadme()
```

# Notes:

A REQUIRED text file, README, SHOULD describe the dataset in more detail. A BIDS dataset
MUST NOT contain more than one README file (with or without extension) at its root
directory. For more details on the README file, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/03-modality-agnostic-files.html#readme>).

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addStimuliFolder
documentation:
Add stimuli in a stimuli directory.

This method adds all stimuli represented in a given tsv file into the `/stimuli` directory.

# Parameters

event_file

: path of the accompanying task event file.

path

: path to the individual experiment stimuli.

# Returns

None

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addStimuliFolder("sub-01_ses-1_task-simple_run-1_events.tsv")
```

# Notes

The stimulus files can be added in a /stimuli directory (under the root directory of the
dataset; with optional subdirectories) AND using a stim_file column in events.tsv mentioning
which stimulus file was used for a given event. For example images/cat03.jpg will be
translated to /stimuli/images/cat03.jpg. For more details on STIMULI file, see
\[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/04-modality-specific-files/05-task-events.html#stimuli>).

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.createDataset
documentation:
Create the rudimentary body of a new dataset.

# Parameters

readme

: Specifies whether a README file should be created.

chg

: Specifies whether a CHANGES file should be created.

lic

: Specifies whether a LICENSE file should be created.

force

: Specifies whether existing files should be overwritten.

# Returns

None

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> handler.createDataset()
```

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.parseLog
documentation:
Extract events from a log file.

This method parses a given log file based on the specified log level and, optionally, a
regex pattern. It then processes and structures these events into a list each adhering to
the BIDSTaskEvent event format.

# Parameters

file

: The file path of the log file.

level

: The level name of the bids task events.

regex

: A regular expression to parse the message string.

# Return

events

: A list of events like presented stimuli or participant responses.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(
...     dataset="/path/to/your/dataset",
...     subject="sub-01",
...     task="simple"
... )
```

```pycon
>>> log_events = handler.parseLog("simple.log", "BIDS")
```

```pycon
>>> for event in log_events:
...     handler.addEvent(event)
```

```pycon
>>> handler.writeTaskEvents(
...     participant_info={'participant_id': handler.subject}
... )
```

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.writeBehEvents
documentation:
Add events of type BIDSBehEvent and participant information to the dataset.

This method uses both the provided BIDSBehEvent events and participant information to
create the necessary `*_beh.tsv` file, while also making updates to the `participant.tsv`
file.

# Parameters

participant_info

: A dictionary describing properties of each participant (such as age, sex, etc.).

# Returns

file_name

: File name of the created tsv event file.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = BIDSHandler(
...     dataset="/path/to/your/dataset",
...     subject="sub-01",
...     task="simple"
... )
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addEvent(BIDSBehEvent(trial=1))
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addEvent(BIDSBehEvent(trial=2))
```

```pycon
>>> handler.writeBehEvents(
...     participant_info={'participant_id': handler.subject}
... )
```

# Notes

For more details on behavioral experiment files, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/04-modality-specific-files/07-behavioral-experiments.html>).

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.writeTaskEvents
documentation:
Add events of type BIDSTaskEvent and participant information to the dataset.

This method uses both the provided BIDSTaskEvent events and participant information to
create the necessary `*_events.tsv` file, while also making updates to the `participant.tsv`
file.

# Parameters

participant_info

: A dictionary describing properties of each participant (such as age, sex, etc.).

# Returns

file_name

: File name of the created tsv event file.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(
...     dataset="/path/to/your/dataset",
...     subject="sub-01",
...     task="simple"
... )
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addEvent(BIDSTaskEvent(onset=1.0, duration=0)
```

```pycon
>>> handler.writeTaskEvents(participant_info={'participant_id': handler.subject})
```

# Notes

For more details on task event files, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/04-modality-specific-files/05-task-events.html>).

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler._writeEvents
documentation:
Add events and information of a participant to the dataset.

This method scans all passed events and information to create the required tabular files.

Parameters
----------
events : list
    A set of structured activities performed by the participant.
participant_info : dict
    A dictionary describing properties of each participant (such as age, sex, etc.).
event_type : str
    The type of events to save. Must be one of "BEH" or "EVENTS".

Returns
-------
file_name : str
    File name of the created tsv event file.

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.events
documentation: Get the list of events.

documentation: A set of neuroimaging and behavioral data acquired for a purpose of a particular study.

A participant identifier of the form sub-<label>, matching a participant entity found in
the dataset.

methods.name: psychopy_bids.bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.acq
documentation: A label to distinguish a different set of parameters used for acquiring the same modality.

A class to handle the creation of a BIDS-compliant dataset.

This class provides methods for creating and managing BIDS datasets and their modality agnostic
files plus modality specific files.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

modules.name: bids
documentation:
This package provides classes that supports the creation of a BIDS-compliant dataset with modality
agnostic files describing the dataset and participants, as well as the creation of modality specific
files describing task events and behavioral experiments.

BIDSTaskEvent class.

modules.name: bids.bidstaskevent
documentation:
This module provides the classes BIDSTaskEvent, BIDSError, OnsetError, DurationError,
TrialTypeError, SampleError, ResponseTimeError, HedError, StimFileError, IdentifierError,
DatabaseError

classes.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a BIDSTaskEvent object.

Parameters
----------
onset : int, float, str
    Onset (in seconds) of the event, measured from the beginning of the acquisition of the
    first data point stored in the corresponding task data file.
duration : int, float, str
    Duration of the event (measured from onset) in seconds.
*args : tuple
    Any arguments that the object's superclass's `__init__` method might require.
trial_type : str, optional
    Primary categorisation of each trial to identify them as instances of the experimental
    conditions.
sample : int, str, optional
    Onset of the event according to the sampling scheme of the recorded modality.
response_time : int, float, str, optional
    Marker value associated with the event.
value : object, optional
    Marker value associated with the event.
hed : str, optional
    Hierarchical Event Descriptor (HED) Tag.
stim_file : str, optional
    Represents the location of the stimulus file (such as an image, video, or audio file)
    presented at the given onset time.
identifier : str, optional
    Represents the database identifier of the stimulus file presented at the given onset
    time.
database : str, optional
    Represents the database of the stimulus file presented at the given onset time.
**kwargs : dict
    Any keyword arguments that the object's superclass's `__init__` method might require.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.__setitem__
documentation:
Set the value associated with a specific key.

If the key already exists in the event, the new value will be validated using
the `_validateAttribute` method before being set.

Parameters
----------
key : str
    The key for which to set the value.
value : object
    The value to be associated with the key.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.__repr__
documentation:
Return a printable representational string of the given object.

This method returns a string representation of the BIDSTaskEvent object containing its
attribute-value pairs.

Returns:
-------
str
    A string representation of the BIDSTaskEvent object.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent._validateOnset
documentation:
Validate and format the onset value.

This method ensures that the onset value is a numeric type or can be successfully converted
to a float. If the value is provided as a string, it must be a numeric string (containing
only digits).

Parameters
----------
value : int, float, str
    The onset value to be validated. Can be provided as an int, float, or numeric string.

Returns
-------
float
    The validated onset value, rounded to four decimal places.

Raises
------
OnsetError
    If the provided onset value is not a valid numeric type or string.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent._validateDuration
documentation:
Validate and process the duration value.

This method checks the provided value and validates whether it represents a valid
duration for an event. Valid duration values include non-negative integers or floats,
as well as the string "n/a". If the provided value is numeric as a string, it is
converted to a float before validation.

Parameters
----------
value : int, float, str
    The value to be validated as a valid event duration.

Returns
-------
float or str
    The processed and validated duration value.

Raises
------
DurationError
    If the provided value is not a valid event duration.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent._validateSample
documentation:
Validate and process the sample value.

This method checks the provided value and validates whether it represents a valid sample
index for an event. Valid sample values include positive integers. If the provided value is
numeric as a string, it is converted to an integer before validation.

Parameters
----------
value : int or str
    The value to be validated as a valid sample index.

Returns
-------
int or None
    The processed and validated sample index, or None if the value is not valid.

Raises
------
SampleError
    If the provided value is not a valid sample index.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent._validateResponseTime
documentation:
Validate and process the response time value.

This method checks the provided value and validates whether it represents a valid response
time for an event. Valid response time values include non-negative integers, floats, as well
as the string "n/a". If the provided value is numeric as a string, it is converted to a
float before validation.

Parameters
----------
value : int or float or str
    The value to be validated as a valid response time.

Returns
-------
float or str or None
    The processed and validated response time value, or None if the value is not valid.

Raises
------
ResponseTimeError
    If the provided value is not a valid response time.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent._validateAttribute
documentation:
Validate and process an attribute value.

This method performs validation on an attribute value. If the value is not None,
it checks whether it's an instance of string. If it's a string, it is returned as-is.
If the value is not a string, an error class corresponding to the key's name is raised.

Parameters
----------
key : str
    The key representing the attribute's name.
value : object
    The value to be validated.

Returns
-------
str or None
    The validated string value or None if the value is None.

Raises
------
AttributeError
    If the provided value is not a valid string attribute.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.onset
documentation:
Onset (in seconds) of the event, measured from the beginning of the acquisition of the
first data point stored in the corresponding task data file.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.duration

conditions.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.sample
documentation: Onset of the event according to the sampling scheme of the recorded modality.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.response_time
documentation: Response time measured in seconds.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.value
documentation: Marker value associated with the event.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.hed
documentation: Hierarchical Event Descriptor (HED) Tag.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.stim_file
documentation:
Represents the location of the stimulus file (such as an image, video, or audio file)
presented at the given onset time.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.identifier
documentation: Represents the database identifier of the stimulus file presented at the given onset time.

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSTaskEvent.database
documentation: Represents the database of the stimulus file presented at the given onset time.

A class representing task events.

This class describes timing and other properties of events recorded during a run. Events are,
for example, stimuli presented to the participant or participant responses.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> event = BIDSTaskEvent(onset=1.0, duration=0)
```

# Notes

For more details on task event files, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/04-modality-specific-files/05-task-events.html>).

classes.name: bids.bidstaskevent.BIDSError
documentation:
Base class for all BIDS-related exceptions.

This class serves as the base exception class for all exceptions related to BIDS (Brain
Imaging Data Structure) specifications and operations.

documentation:

methods.name: bids.bidstaskevent.OnsetError.__str__
documentation:
Return a string representation of the error.

Returns
-------
str
    A string containing the onset value and the error message.

Exception raised when onset value is incorrect.

This exception is raised when the provided onset value does not meet the expected criteria
for correctness according to BIDS (Brain Imaging Data Structure) specifications.

modules.name: bids.bidshandler
documentation: This module provides the class BIDSHandler

classes.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a BIDSHandler object.

Parameters
----------
dataset : str
    A set of neuroimaging and behavioral data acquired for a purpose of a particular study.
subject : str, optional
    A person or animal participating in the study.
task : str, optional
    A set of structured activities performed by the participant.
session : str, optional
    A logical grouping of neuroimaging and behavioral data consistent across subjects.
data_type : str, optional
    A functional group of different types of data.
acq : str, optional
    Custom label to distinguish different conditions present during multiple runs of the
    same task.
runs : bool, optional
    If True, run will be added to filename.

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.__del__
documentation:
Perform cleanup operations when the object is about to be destroyed.

This method is automatically called when the object is garbage collected or destroyed. It
is responsible for performing cleanup actions such as creating a dataset, writing events to
files, adding sidecar metadata, and adding a stimuli folder.

# Note

> This method should be used with caution as the timing of its execution
> is not guaranteed and it may not be invoked in certain circumstances.

# Raises

> KeyError: If there is an issue with accessing dictionary keys during the cleanup
> process.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> del handler
```

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addEvent
documentation:
Add an event to the list of events.

Parameters:

: event: Any - The event to be added to the list.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler.addEvent(BIDSBehEvent(trial=1))
```

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addChanges
documentation:
Update the version history of the dataset.

This method updates the CPAN changelog-like file `CHANGES` by adding a new version entry
with the specified changes and incrementing the version number accordingly.

# Parameters

changes

: List of changes or bullet points for the new version.

version

: The version part to increment. Must be one of "MAJOR", "MINOR", or "PATCH".

force

: Specifies whether existing file should be overwritten.

# Returns

None

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(
...     dataset="/path/to/your/dataset/",
...     subject=None,
...     task=None
... )
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addChanges(["Added new data files"], "MAJOR")
```

# Notes

Version history of the dataset (describing changes, updates and corrections) MAY be provided
in the form of a CHANGES text file. This file MUST follow the CPAN Changelog convention. The
CHANGES file MUST be either in ASCII or UTF-8 encoding. For more details on the CHANGES
file, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/03-modality-agnostic-files.html#changes>).

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addDatasetDescription
documentation:
Add a description to the dataset.

This method adds the required `dataset_description.json` file to the dataset.

# Parameters

force

: Specifies whether existing files should be overwritten.

# Returns

None

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addDatasetDescription()
```

# Notes:

The file `dataset_description.json` is a JSON file describing the dataset. Every dataset
MUST include this file. For more details, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/03-modality-agnostic-files.html#dataset-description>).

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addJSONSidecar
documentation:
Add a JSON sidecar file.

This method adds an accompanying JSON sidecar file to support tabular data file
documentation.

# Parameters

event_file

: The path of the accompanying task event file.

existing_file

: The path to an existing sidecar JSON file.

version

: The software version used in the experiment.

# Return

file_name

: The path of the created json event file.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addJSONSidecar("sub-01_ses-1_task-simple_run-1_events.tsv")
```

# Notes:

All tabular data files MAY be accompanied by a JSON file describing the columns in detail.
For more details on tabular files, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/02-common-principles.html#tabular-files>).

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addLicense
documentation:
Add a license file to the dataset.

This method downloads a license with the given identifier from the SPDX license list and
copies the content into the file `LICENSE`.

# Parameters

identifier

: Identifier of the license.

force

: Specifies whether existing file should be overwritten.

# Returns

None

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addLicense("CC-BY-NC-4.0")
```

# Notes:

A LICENSE file MAY be provided in addition to the short specification of the used license in
the dataset_description.json "License" field. The "License" field and LICENSE file MUST
correspond. The LICENSE file MUST be either in ASCII or UTF-8 encoding. For more details on
the LICENSE file, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/03-modality-agnostic-files.html#license>).

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addReadme
documentation:
Add a text file explaining the dataset in detail.

This method adds a `README` template file to the dataset, which contains the main sections
needed to describe the dataset in more detail.

# Parameters

force

: Specifies whether existing file should be overwritten.

# Returns

None

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addReadme()
```

# Notes:

A REQUIRED text file, README, SHOULD describe the dataset in more detail. A BIDS dataset
MUST NOT contain more than one README file (with or without extension) at its root
directory. For more details on the README file, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/03-modality-agnostic-files.html#readme>).

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.addStimuliFolder
documentation:
Add stimuli in a stimuli directory.

This method adds all stimuli represented in a given tsv file into the `/stimuli` directory.

# Parameters

event_file

: path of the accompanying task event file.

path

: path to the individual experiment stimuli.

# Returns

None

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addStimuliFolder("sub-01_ses-1_task-simple_run-1_events.tsv")
```

# Notes

The stimulus files can be added in a /stimuli directory (under the root directory of the
dataset; with optional subdirectories) AND using a stim_file column in events.tsv mentioning
which stimulus file was used for a given event. For example images/cat03.jpg will be
translated to /stimuli/images/cat03.jpg. For more details on STIMULI file, see
\[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/04-modality-specific-files/05-task-events.html#stimuli>).

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.createDataset
documentation:
Create the rudimentary body of a new dataset.

# Parameters

readme

: Specifies whether a README file should be created.

chg

: Specifies whether a CHANGES file should be created.

lic

: Specifies whether a LICENSE file should be created.

force

: Specifies whether existing files should be overwritten.

# Returns

None

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```

```pycon
>>> handler.createDataset()
```

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.parseLog
documentation:
Extract events from a log file.

This method parses a given log file based on the specified log level and, optionally, a
regex pattern. It then processes and structures these events into a list each adhering to
the BIDSTaskEvent event format.

# Parameters

file

: The file path of the log file.

level

: The level name of the bids task events.

regex

: A regular expression to parse the message string.

# Return

events

: A list of events like presented stimuli or participant responses.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(
...     dataset="/path/to/your/dataset",
...     subject="sub-01",
...     task="simple"
... )
```

```pycon
>>> log_events = handler.parseLog("simple.log", "BIDS")
```

```pycon
>>> for event in log_events:
...     handler.addEvent(event)
```

```pycon
>>> handler.writeTaskEvents(
...     participant_info={'participant_id': handler.subject}
... )
```

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.writeBehEvents
documentation:
Add events of type BIDSBehEvent and participant information to the dataset.

This method uses both the provided BIDSBehEvent events and participant information to
create the necessary `*_beh.tsv` file, while also making updates to the `participant.tsv`
file.

# Parameters

participant_info

: A dictionary describing properties of each participant (such as age, sex, etc.).

# Returns

file_name

: File name of the created tsv event file.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = BIDSHandler(
...     dataset="/path/to/your/dataset",
...     subject="sub-01",
...     task="simple"
... )
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addEvent(BIDSBehEvent(trial=1))
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addEvent(BIDSBehEvent(trial=2))
```

```pycon
>>> handler.writeBehEvents(
...     participant_info={'participant_id': handler.subject}
... )
```

# Notes

For more details on behavioral experiment files, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/04-modality-specific-files/07-behavioral-experiments.html>).

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.writeTaskEvents
documentation:
Add events of type BIDSTaskEvent and participant information to the dataset.

This method uses both the provided BIDSTaskEvent events and participant information to
create the necessary `*_events.tsv` file, while also making updates to the `participant.tsv`
file.

# Parameters

participant_info

: A dictionary describing properties of each participant (such as age, sex, etc.).

# Returns

file_name

: File name of the created tsv event file.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = bids.BIDSHandler(
...     dataset="/path/to/your/dataset",
...     subject="sub-01",
...     task="simple"
... )
```

```pycon
>>> handler.addEvent(BIDSTaskEvent(onset=1.0, duration=0)
```

```pycon
>>> handler.writeTaskEvents(participant_info={'participant_id': handler.subject})
```

# Notes

For more details on task event files, see \[BIDS Specification\](<https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/04-modality-specific-files/05-task-events.html>).

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler._writeEvents
documentation:
Add events and information of a participant to the dataset.

This method scans all passed events and information to create the required tabular files.

Parameters
----------
events : list
    A set of structured activities performed by the participant.
participant_info : dict
    A dictionary describing properties of each participant (such as age, sex, etc.).
event_type : str
    The type of events to save. Must be one of "BEH" or "EVENTS".

Returns
-------
file_name : str
    File name of the created tsv event file.

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.events
documentation: Get the list of events.

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.dataset
documentation: A set of neuroimaging and behavioral data acquired for a purpose of a particular study.

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.subject
documentation:
A participant identifier of the form sub-<label>, matching a participant entity found in
the dataset.

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.task
documentation: A set of structured activities performed by the participant.

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.session
documentation: A logical grouping of neuroimaging and behavioral data consistent across subjects.

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.data_type
documentation: A functional group of different types of data.

methods.name: bids.bidshandler.BIDSHandler.acq
documentation: A label to distinguish a different set of parameters used for acquiring the same modality.

A class to handle the creation of a BIDS-compliant dataset.

This class provides methods for creating and managing BIDS datasets and their modality agnostic
files plus modality specific files.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> handler = BIDSHandler(dataset="/path/to/your/dataset")
```
